text: The UUIS shall provide a Web interface that allows users to access and manage the integrated inventory.
The IUFA shall guarantee secure access to the data from outside the university during working hours.
The UUIS shall allow users to transfer assets.
The UUIS shall allow users to edit assets.
The UUIS shall allow users to modify all asset fields except for IDs.
The UUIS shall allow the use of a bulk entry file for modifying assets.
The UUIS shall allow users to add inventory assets.
The UUIS shall allow the use of bulk entry to add multiple assets.
The UUIS shall allow authorized users to create requests to borrow an asset or reserve a space.
The UUIS shall allow inventory users to check returned assets and update the inventory.
The UUIS shall allow IT group members to create a new space and modify floor structures upon receipt of an exception request.
The UUIS shall allow authorized users to display and approve pending requests.
The UUIS shall notify the user by email when a request is treated.
The UUIS shall update the inventory when a user receives a requested asset.
The UUIS shall authenticate all users via a username and a password.
The UUIS shall allow administrative level users to delegate authorized actions to other users.
The UUIS shall generate asset reports by location.
The UUIS shall generate request reports.
The UUIS shall generate user permission reports.
The UUIS shall enable a learning time between 2 to 4 hours maximum for users.
The Web interface shall use consistent terminology for users with basic internet and office experience.
The application shall be available during all working hours.
The application shall be installable on Microsoft and Unix platforms.
The Web application shall run on IE, Firefox, Chrome, Opera, and Safari browsers.
The UUIS shall restrict database server access to IT team members locally.
The UUIS shall terminate any query that exceeds 1 minute of execution time.
The UUIS shall execute backup operations periodically.
The UUIS shall be designed to facilitate future evolution and maintenance operations.
